summary: Iter-4 refactor regression: validated the code-quality / security refactor (pickle->joblib, random->numpy Generator, risk-engine ladder lookup, function decomposition in server.py/ai_service.py/ml_drift.py). Executed full backend pytest suite across phase1+phase2+phase3 plus a new iter4 refactor-focused file. 69/69 tests pass against the public URL. Banned-imports grep clean, ML model loads from default_model.joblib (AUC ~0.83), all 6 ladder branches map correctly (95>=90, 90>=80, 85>=70, 80>=60, 75>=50, 70<50 floor), drift+retrain still work, contract download honours bearer + ?auth= query and returns 401/401/403/404 for missing/invalid/foreign/unknown.
critical_code_review_comments: risk_engine.ADVANCE_LADDER tuple-of-tuples is clean; _lookup_advance_terms iterates once and falls through to the (0, 70.0, 8.0) floor. The inline comment correctly calls out the final row as the unreachable-but-safe guard. Good.
risk_engine no longer references undefined advance_rate/discount_fee_rate — the lookup assigns both atomically via tuple unpacking. Undefined-variable warning resolved.

ml_service._load_from_disk still carries a LEGACY_PICKLE_PATH branch (loads via joblib.load, which internally can handle pickle). Since the legacy .pkl was deleted and spec says 'unused currently', consider gating this behind an env flag or removing in a later cleanup pass — current behaviour is fine and backwards-compatible, but note that joblib.load on an arbitrary .pkl still re-opens the RCE surface if a malicious file is dropped in /app/backend. Low risk because the path is hard-coded and write-protected in the container.

server.py startup() is now a slim orchestrator calling _seed_brands/_seed_admin/_seed_demo_creator/_preload_ml_model/_init_object_storage — easy to unit-test individually and easy to reason about boot order. Good.
ai_service.analyze_contract_with_ai now composes _build_user_prompt -> _call_claude -> _parse_json_payload. Each helper is <40 lines and has a single responsibility. Good.

ml_drift.retrain_from_production decomposition into _production_feature_frame / _labeled_production_frame / _build_training_frame / _fit_pipeline is clean and reads top-to-bottom. The sample-weight blending (prod weight 0.7) is explicit and testable.

download_contract / _extract_bearer_token / _user_from_token: clear separation of auth-token extraction (header OR ?auth= query) from authZ owner/admin check. 401 vs 403 vs 404 boundaries confirmed by test.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase2_api.py (test scaffold only: accept 'pending' as valid Stripe session state alongside 'unpaid'/'paid'/'no_payment_required')
seed_data_creation: Created ~3 TEST_iter4_* deals (analyze flow + 1 uploaded 'iter4_contract.pdf'). Registered one ephemeral foreign creator (iter4_other_<hex>@mypay.io) for the 403 cross-tenant download check. Triggered one retrain cycle (AUC observed 0.828) and one drift report call.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-4 refactor verified. New file /app/backend/tests/test_iter4_refactor.py covers banned-imports grep, default_model.joblib presence + legacy .pkl absence, /api/ml/status shape ({available, roc_auc, feature_names[>=8]}), /api/deals/{id}/analyze risk.ml sub-block keys (default_prob, survival_prob, ml_score, model_auc), risk_engine._lookup_advance_terms ladder for score buckets (95/85/75/65/55/45/20 -> 95/90/85/80/75/70/70 advance_pct), drift+retrain roundtrip, and 6 contract download paths (bearer 200, ?auth= query 200, missing token 401, bad JWT 401, foreign creator 403, missing contract 404). The only test-scaffold tweak outside the new file: test_phase2_api.py::test_payment_status_polling now accepts 'pending' as a valid intermediate Stripe status (reality differs from old Stripe test-mode docs). DealCreate schema uses deal_title + deal_amount + payment_terms_days (NOT amount_total/net_days). risk_engine._lookup_advance_terms returns tuple (advance_pct, fee_pct) in percentage units (95.0, not 0.95). ML status endpoint returns top-level {available: true, roc_auc, n_train, n_test, n_production, default_rate, feature_names}. No action items for main agent.
rca of the issue: No open issues. Three scaffold issues I fixed while authoring iter4 tests: (1) assumed fraction advance rate vs actual percentage (95.0 vs 0.95) — fixed; (2) assumed /ml/status shape {model_loaded, model_auc} vs actual {available, roc_auc} — fixed; (3) assumed DealCreate fields amount_total/net_days vs actual deal_title/deal_amount/payment_terms_days — fixed. Also accepted Stripe 'pending' status in phase2 polling test (pre-existing flake, unrelated to refactor).
